danger_signals.visual_form: Ducks/clay pigeons flying off-screen without being shot
danger_signals.meaning: Missed shot; round progress threatened; failure state approaching

danger_signals.visual_form: Targets moving faster across screen in successive rounds
danger_signals.meaning: Difficulty escalation; higher skill requirement; approaching failure threshold

danger_signals.visual_form: Two ducks appearing together (Game B) or three targets in arcade version
danger_signals.meaning: Increased threat density; narrower margin for error; resource pressure (limited shots per round)

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen transition indicating failure to meet minimum hit quota
danger_signals.meaning: Round threshold not met; end of session

safety_signals.visual_form: Target disappears when struck by light gun crosshair
safety_signals.meaning: Correct action completed; points awarded; round progress secured

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition to next numbered round after meeting target quota
safety_signals.meaning: Minimum threshold achieved; player safe to continue

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual score reward for completing all ten targets in single round
safety_signals.meaning: Perfect execution acknowledged; optional mastery reward

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible attempt counter (three shots per round)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; player agency maintained as long as attempts remain

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: First-person perspective; screen center focus for aiming

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Comedic hunting companion; jumps into frame during bonus stages
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: In arcade bonus stage, dog becomes distraction target (shootable but scoreable as failure); removed from console version for 'family-friendly' positioning
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Grass field environment; appears during bonus rounds as ally/obstacle hybrid

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ducks (flying targets) vs. clay pigeons (stationary launch targets)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ducks show varied flight patterns; clay pigeons arc predictably; arcade version shows haphazard movement in Round 0 kill screen
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Open field/sky in duck modes; distant field perspective in clay pigeon mode (Game C)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single or dual targets appearing at moderate speed; clear sightlines; generous hit windows
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ducks/pigeons follow predictable flight arcs; appear in center-to-medium screen areas; disappear slowly

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Targets accelerate; multiple simultaneous targets increase frequency; hit windows narrow
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ducks fly faster; clay pigeons launch with greater arc velocity; targets spend less time on-screen

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rapid-fire targets; dense screen occupation; minimal safe space between targets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Targets move near-maximum speed; multiple overlapping flight paths; near-impossible hit quotas

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Kill screen (Round 0, post-99 completion in Game A)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual glitches; haphazard target placement; erratic behavior; fractured screen logic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Targets appear and disappear unpredictably; hit zones become unreliable; game communicates 'system breakdown' through visual chaos

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Arcade bonus stage (every 2 rounds in Vs. Duck Hunt)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grass field environment; hunting dog prominently displayed; multiple ducks burst from grass simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dog jumps into line of fire as distraction mechanic; shooting dog ends bonus stage and penalizes player; frames dog as both ally and obstacle

environmental_storytelling.example: Game A/B show open sky environment (duck hunting); Game C shows distant field perspective with clay pigeons launching upward (sport shooting). Visuals establish narrative context without dialogue.
Round progression increases target quantity and speed, visually communicating escalating challenge through screen occupation rather than explicit messaging.

Hunting dog in arcade bonus stages establishes rural/hunting scenario flavor; dog's mocking reaction to being shot (arcade) or absence (console) communicates tone difference between versions without story exposition.
Clay pigeon mode (Game C) shifts perspective to distant field view, visually distinguishing shooting sport from duck hunting through spatial framing alone.
Round 0 intentionally breaks visual logic (erratic target placement, glitched behavior) to communicate 'system failure' or 'natural endpoint.' Visual breakdown communicates narrative closure.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appears singly or in pairs; flies in wide arcs; remains on-screen for extended periods; predictable trajectory
Rapid flight speed; erratic path changes; minimal on-screen duration; appears in cluster formations
Launches in gentle arc from lower-left area; follows ballistic trajectory; long flight duration before exit
Launches with steep velocity; minimal arc duration; exits screen rapidly; multiple simultaneous launches
Remains in grass; jumps into firing line as distraction; reacts negatively if shot (scolds player); communicates dual role as companion and obstacle
Targets spawn unpredictably; hitboxes become unreliable; visual glitches manifest; communicates system has reached failure state or natural conclusion

notes: Duck Hunt is a minimal-narrative light gun game that communicates world-state almost entirely through visual mechanics and behavioral patterns. Danger/safety signals are implicit in target visibility, speed, and count rather than explicit UI. The hunting dog in arcade versions serves as the only recurring 'character' element and represents narrative/tone difference between arcade (player can shoot dog; dog reacts) and console (dog immune to shots; family-friendly framing). Environmental storytelling relies on perspective shifts (sky vs. distant field) and mode-specific visual contexts (hunting vs. sport shooting). The kill screen represents unusual deliberate use of visual glitching to communicate narrative endpoint. No faction dynamics or political situations exist in this game; all signaling is difficulty/challenge-focused.
